title: papercheck
text: can install development version papercheck GitHub :

title: papercheck
text: function pdf2grobid() can read PDF files save TEI format created grobid. requires internet connection takes seconds per paper, done results saved later use. server unavailable, can use grobid web interface.
code: grobid_dir <- system.file("grobid", package="papercheck") pdf_file <- file.path(grobid_dir, "incest.pdf") xml_file <- pdf2grobid(pdf_file)

title: papercheck
text: function read_grobid() can read XML files parsed grobid.

title: papercheck
text: can access parsed table full text paper via s$full_text, may find convenient use function search_text(). defaults return data table sentence, section type, header, div, paragraph sentence numbers, file name. (section type best guess headers, may always accurate.)

title: papercheck
text: can search specific word phrase setting pattern argument. pattern regex string default; set fixed = TRUE want find exact text matches.

title: papercheck
text: Set return one “sentence”, “paragraph”, “section”, “match” control gets returned.

title: papercheck
text: can also return just matched text regex search setting return = "match". extra ... arguments search_text() passed grep(), perl = TRUE allows use complex regex, like .
code: pattern <- "[^\\s\\(\\)]+\\s*(=|<)\\s*[0-9\\.-]+" text <- search_text(s, pattern, return = "match", perl = TRUE)

title: papercheck
text: functions pdf2grobid() read_grobid() also work folder files, returning list XML file paths scienceverse study objects, respectively. search_text() also works list study objects.
code: grobid_dir <- system.file("grobid", package="papercheck")  studies <- read_grobid(grobid_dir)  hypotheses <- search_text(studies, "hypothesi",                            section = "intro",                            return = "paragraph")

title: papercheck
code: module_list() #>              name                 title type #> 1    ai-summarise    Summarise Sections   ai #> 2    all-p-values     List All P-Values text #> 3        marginal Marginal Significance text #> 4       osf-links       Check OSF Links code #> 5 retractionwatch       RetractionWatch code #>                                                                      path #> 1    /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/papercheck/modules/ai-summarise.json #> 2    /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/papercheck/modules/all-p-values.json #> 3        /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/papercheck/modules/marginal.json #> 4       /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/papercheck/modules/osf-links.json #> 5 /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/papercheck/modules/retractionwatch.json p <- module_run(studies, "all-p-values")  head(p) #> # A tibble: 6 × 7 #>   text      section header     div     p     s file         #>   <chr>     <chr>   <chr>    <dbl> <dbl> <int> <chr>        #> 1 p = 0.012 intro   [div-01]     1     4     6 eyecolor.xml #> 2 p = 0.849 intro   [div-01]     1     4     7 eyecolor.xml #> 3 p = 0.019 intro   [div-01]     1     4     8 eyecolor.xml #> 4 p = 0.879 intro   [div-01]     1     4     9 eyecolor.xml #> 5 p = 0.266 intro   [div-01]     1     4    11 eyecolor.xml #> 6 p = 0.403 intro   [div-01]     1     4    13 eyecolor.xml

title: papercheck
text: can query extracted text papers chatGPT.

title: papercheck
text: Run need provide API key. can enter manually call gpt(), add following lines .Renviron file user project.
code: gpt_setup() CHATGPT_KEY="sk-proj-abcdefghijklmnopqrs0123456789ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRS" RETICULATE_PYTHON="~/.virtualenvs/r-reticulate/bin/python" # useful if you aren't sure where this file is usethis::edit_r_environ()

title: papercheck
code: query <- "What is the hypothesis of this study?" gpt_hypo <- gpt(hypotheses, query)

title: Authors and Citation
text: Lisa DeBruine. Author, maintainer. Daniel Lakens. Author.

title: Authors and Citation
text: DeBruine L, Lakens D (2024). papercheck: Check Scientific Papers Best Practices. R package version 0.0.0.9013, https://scienceverse.github.io/papercheck/, https://github.com/debruine/papercheck.
code: @Manual{,   title = {papercheck: Check Scientific Papers for Best Practices},   author = {Lisa DeBruine and Daniel Lakens},   year = {2024},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9013, https://scienceverse.github.io/papercheck/},   url = {https://github.com/debruine/papercheck}, }

what: Check Scientific Papers for Best Practices
title: Check Scientific Papers for Best Practices
text: goal papercheck automatically check scientific papers best practices.

title: Check Scientific Papers for Best Practices
text: can install development version papercheck GitHub :

title: Check Scientific Papers for Best Practices
code: library(papercheck) grobid_dir <- system.file("grobid", package="papercheck") filename <- file.path(grobid_dir, "incest.xml") s <- read_grobid(filename) pattern <- "[^\\s\\(\\)]+\\s*(=|<)\\s*[0-9\\.-]+" text <- search_text(s, pattern, return = "match", perl = TRUE)

title: Check Scientific Papers for Best Practices
code: # read in all the XML files in this directory studies <- read_grobid(grobid_dir)  # select paragraphs in the intros containing the text "hypothesi" hypotheses <- search_text(studies, "hypothesi",                            section = "intro",                            return = "paragraph")

title: Check Scientific Papers for Best Practices
code: # ask chatGPT a question query <- "What is the hypothesis of this study?" gpt_hypo <- gpt(hypotheses, query, group_by = "file")

title: Check Scientific Papers for Best Practices
code: statcheck <- stats(studies)  check <- statcheck |>   dplyr::filter(error == TRUE) |>   dplyr::select(file, computed_p:one_tailed_in_txt) p <- check_p_values(studies)

title: Check p-values — check_p_values
code: filename <- system.file("grobid", "incest.xml", package="papercheck") study <- read_grobid(filename) check_p_values(study) #>   p_comp reported_p p_decimals #> 1      =      0.019          3 #> 2      =      0.005          3 #> 3      =      0.044          3 #> 4      =      0.055          3 #> 5      =      0.170          2 #>                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              text #> 1                                                                                                                                   Our main, omnibus analysis showed a significant three-way interaction among incest type, participant sex, and sibling type (Estimate = 0.285, t(578.003) = 2.359, p = 0.019). #> 2                                                                                                                      Our analysis of moral wrongness scores for sibling incest scores showed a significant interaction between participant sex and sibling type (Estimate = -0.559, t(578) = -2.84, p = 0.005). #> 3 Women with brothers only tended to view sibling incest more negatively than did women with sisters only (Estimate = 0.179, t(452) = 2.022, p = 0.044) while men with brothers only tended to view sibling incest less negatively than did men with sisters only (Estimate = -0.38, t(126) = -1.933, p = 0.055). #> 4 Women with brothers only tended to view sibling incest more negatively than did women with sisters only (Estimate = 0.179, t(452) = 2.022, p = 0.044) while men with brothers only tended to view sibling incest less negatively than did men with sisters only (Estimate = -0.38, t(126) = -1.933, p = 0.055). #> 5                                                                                                       In contrast, our analysis of moral wrongness scores for parental incest scores showed no significant interaction between participant sex and sibling type (Estimate = -0.268, t(578) = -1.373, p = 0.17). #>   section  header div p s       file imprecise #> 1 results Results   5 1 1 incest.xml     FALSE #> 2 results Results   5 2 1 incest.xml     FALSE #> 3 results Results   5 2 2 incest.xml     FALSE #> 4 results Results   5 2 2 incest.xml     FALSE #> 5 results Results   5 2 3 incest.xml     FALSE

title: Concatenate tables — concat_tables
text: Concatenate tables across list scienceverse objects

title: Concatenate tables — concat_tables

title: Concatenate tables — concat_tables
code: grobid_dir <- system.file("grobid", package = "papercheck") studies <- read_grobid(grobid_dir) references <- concat_tables(studies, c("refs", "references"))

title: Query ChatGPT — gpt
text: Ask ChatGPT question want text search_text()

title: Query ChatGPT — gpt
code: gpt(   text,   query,   context = "You are a scientist",   text_col = "text",   group_by = "file",   CHATGPT_KEY = Sys.getenv("CHATGPT_KEY"),   gpt_model = "gpt-3.5-turbo-1106",   chunk_size = 500,   chunk_overlap = 100,   temperature = 0,   include_query = FALSE )

title: Query ChatGPT — gpt
text: text text send chatGPT (vector strings, data frame text column) query query ask chatGPT context context send chatGPT text_col name text column text data frame group_by column(s) group text data frame CHATGPT_KEY API key ChatGPT gpt_model model name pass ChatOpenAI chunk_size text chunk size embeddings chunk_overlap overlap text chunks embeddings temperature temperature value ChatGPT (0.0 2.0) include_query Whether include query context strings returned table

title: Query ChatGPT — gpt
text: need use ChatGPT API key. avoid type ou, add .Renviron file following format (can use usethis::edit_r_environ() access .Renviron file) CHATGPT_KEY="key_value_asdf"

title: Less scary green messages — message
text: ... message components (see message) domain (see message) appendLF append new line? (see message)

title: Search text — search_text
text: Search text study list study objects. Also works table results search_text() call.

title: Search text — search_text
code: search_text(   study,   pattern = ".*",   section = NULL,   return = c("sentence", "paragraph", "section", "match"),   ignore.case = TRUE,   ... )

title: Search text — search_text
text: study study object created read_grobid list study objects pattern regex pattern search section section(s) search return kind text return, full sentence, paragraph, section text , just (regex) match ignore.case whether ignore case text searching ... additional arguments pass grep() regexpr(), fixed = TRUE

title: Search text — search_text
code: filename <- system.file("grobid", "eyecolor.xml", package="papercheck") study <- read_grobid(filename)  search_text(study, "p\\s*(=|<)\\s*[0-9\\.]+", return = "match") #> # A tibble: 14 × 7 #>    text      section header              div     p     s file         #>    <chr>     <chr>   <chr>             <dbl> <dbl> <int> <chr>        #>  1 p = 0.012 intro   [div-01]              1     4     6 eyecolor.xml #>  2 p = 0.849 intro   [div-01]              1     4     7 eyecolor.xml #>  3 p = 0.019 intro   [div-01]              1     4     8 eyecolor.xml #>  4 p = 0.879 intro   [div-01]              1     4     9 eyecolor.xml #>  5 p = 0.266 intro   [div-01]              1     4    11 eyecolor.xml #>  6 p = 0.403 intro   [div-01]              1     4    13 eyecolor.xml #>  7 p = 0.13  intro   [div-01]              1     4    14 eyecolor.xml #>  8 p = 0.337 intro   [div-01]              1     4    15 eyecolor.xml #>  9 p = 0.1   intro   [div-01]              1     4    16 eyecolor.xml #> 10 p = 0.029 method  Statistical tests     5     1    13 eyecolor.xml #> 11 p = 0.027 method  Statistical tests     5     1    14 eyecolor.xml #> 12 p = 0.076 method  Statistical tests     5     1    16 eyecolor.xml #> 13 p = 0.091 method  Statistical tests     5     1    18 eyecolor.xml #> 14 p = 0.196 method  Statistical tests     5     1    19 eyecolor.xml

1         t  NA 578.003         =      2.359      =      0.019 0.018655985 #> 2         t  NA 578.000         =     -2.840      =      0.005 0.004670184 #> 3         t  NA 452.000         =      2.022      =      0.044 0.043764941 #> 4         t  NA 126.000         =     -1.933      =      0.055 0.055479644 #> 5         t  NA 578.000         =     -1.373      =      0.170 0.170284762 #> FALSE #> 3     t(452) = 2.022, p = 0.044 FALSE          FALSE             FALSE #> 4    t(126) = -1.933, p = 0.055 FALSE          FALSE             FALSE #> 5     t(578) = -1.373, p = 0.17 FALSE          FALSE             FALSE #>   apa_factor #> 1          1 #> 2          1 #> 3          1 #> 4          1 #> 5          1 #> significant three-way interaction among incest type, participant sex, and sibling type (Estimate = 0.285, t(578.003) = 2.359, p = 0.019). #> 2                                                                                                                      Our analysis of moral wrongness scores for sibling incest scores showed a significant interaction between participant sex and sibling type (Estimate = -0.559, t(578) = -2.84, p = 0.005). #> 3 Women with brothers only tended to view sibling incest more negatively than did women with sisters only (Estimate = 0.179, t(452) = 2.022, p = 0.044) while men with brothers only tended to view sibling incest less negatively than did men with sisters only (Estimate = -0.38, t(126) = -1.933, p = 0.055). #> 4 Women with brothers only tended to
